MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar May-21-2015 Assigned To Trial Court

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Defendant Moore Mccormack Resources, Inc.
Off Calendar Jan-14-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Maisie C. Sokolove of KNOX RICKSEN, LLP (510-285-2500), appearing for defendant / moving party Moore McCormack Resources, Inc.
Gilbert L. Purcell of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Mary Geiger, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT MOORE MCCORMACK RESOURCES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Moore McCormack Resources, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. The Certificates of Discharge create a triable issue whether Mr. Taylor was aboard defendant's ship, a fact which defendant does not dispute. The Court agrees with plaintiff that whether Mr. Taylor was aboard defendant's ship was the only issue raised by defendant's undisputed material facts laid out in its Separate Statement. Therefore the Court did not consider any issue other than that. Additionally, the Court treated this motion as solely a motion for summary judgment. Defendant's Notice of Motion and Separate Statement failed to specifically and separately state each cause of action sought to be summarily adjudicated, in violation of CRC 3.1350. Plaintiffs to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11596 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Defendant Moore Mccormack Resources, Inc.
